story_title: Iran–US war and Strait of Hormuz blockade
tldr: Headlines split three ways: most buckets lead with the military blockade threat (SECURITY_DEFENSE); energy and commodity reporters foreground the supply shock (ECONOMIC); and Anglophone and opinion outlets frame the crisis through Trump's China leverage deficit (POLITICAL). Ukraine alone runs a tangential fossil-fuel revenue headline and appears as a silence.

evidence.quote: to join efforts to reopen strait of Hormuz
Blockade of Strait of Hormuz not in common interests
Iran, Oman hold talks on Strait of Hormuz shipping safety
Iran retains access to majority of missile launch sites

frames.sub_frame: energy price shock and supply disruption

evidence.quote: oil, kerosine shortages if Iran war continues
shock offerta senza precedenti con blocco Hormuz
Soaring inflation and plummeting economy test Iran
oil spikes as Hormuz remains closed

frames.sub_frame: Trump-Xi summit and US leverage deficit

evidence.quote: China urges Pakistan to intensify Iran-US mediation
America Has Lost Its Leverage Over China
Iran War Casts Shadow Over Trump's High-Stakes China Trip

bottom_line: The Hormuz crisis headline layer separates cleanly by beat: security correspondents lead with the blockade, commodity desks lead with energy prices, and political reporters lead with Trump's weakened position entering the Xi summit. Ukraine's only headline is a tangential fossil-fuel revenue story, making it a topical silence.
